content.full_text:
August 14, 1990

Grace N. Mitchell  
Superintendent-President  
Cuesta College  
P.O. Box 8106  
San Luis Obispo, CA 93403-8106

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance  
Our File No. I-90-479

Dear Ms. Mitchell:

We have received your letter inquiring about the conflict-of-interest provisions that may pertain to Laurence L. Laurent, member of your board of trustees and candidate for San Luis Obispo County Supervisor.

The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 While the Act does have prohibitions on conflicts of interests (Section 87100), those prohibitions focus on a public official's private financial interests and do not appear to apply to the situation your letter describes. (See Section 87103.) The situation that you describe may implicate the "doctrine of incompatible public offices." However that doctrine is not a part of the Act, and the Commission has no jurisdiction to interpret or enforce it. You should consult with the Office of the California Attorney General for advice on the doctrine. (I have enclosed a copy of an Attorney General opinion discussing the doctrine for your information.)

I trust that this letter has provided you with the guidance

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Mark Morodomi
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MM:dg
